evals.name: New APIView follows DRF conventions
evals.prompt: Add a DRF endpoint to list and create the current user's projects. A user may own at most 10 projects. Follow our DRF conventions.
evals.expected_output: A view (APIView or generics.*) with explicit permission_classes, serializer validation via is_valid(raise_exception=True) or a generic view, status via rest_framework.status constants, and the 10-project limit enforced by raising ValidationError(code=...) in the serializer or perform_create — never a hand-built Response dict, never validation in the view body.

evals.name: Domain error uses a custom APIException with an UPPER_SNAKE_CASE code
evals.prompt: We need to reject requests to cancel an order that has already shipped, with HTTP 409. Add the error following our conventions so the frontend can localize it.
evals.expected_output: A custom APIException subclass with status_code = 409 and default_code = 'ORDER_ALREADY_SHIPPED' (UPPER_SNAKE_CASE), raised rather than returned. Notes that the unified exception handler renders it as {"error": "CODE", "detail": "..."}.

assertions.instruction: Verify the error code is UPPER_SNAKE_CASE and the answer raises a custom APIException subclass rather than returning Response({...}, status=409) manually.

evals.name: Review a view that bypasses the handler and validates in the view
evals.expected_output: Flags: (1) validation logic lives in the view instead of a serializer; (2) hand-built Response({...}, status=400) bypasses the unified exception handler and lacks an UPPER_SNAKE_CASE error code; (3) bare integer status instead of status.HTTP_* constants; (4) missing explicit permission_classes. Recommends moving validation into a serializer with is_valid(raise_exception=True) and raising ValidationError / a custom APIException with code= rather than returning dicts.

assertions.instruction: Verify the review flags BOTH the hand-built Response(...) envelope bypass (no error code, bare integer status) AND that validation belongs in a serializer, and recommends raising with an UPPER_SNAKE_CASE code.
